title: Preparation of human Red Blood Cells for Scanning Electron Microscopy imaging
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ee5i8","text":"SEM imaging can be used to quantify RBC shape. RBCs are sampled from fresh blood samples or from packed RBC units during storage. RBCs are resuspended into PBS to make up 500 μL of solution at 5% haematocrit. RBCs are then fixed by progressively adding a concentrated 2% glutaraldehyde solution in the cell suspension. The cells are incubated for 30 min at RT and in the dark, before being centrifuged and washed in PBS. This fixation protocol was established in order to limit RBC shape changes due to the presence of glutaraldehyde.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ejd9s","text":"After fixation, a RBC suspension at 2.5% haematocrit is adhered to coverslips coated with poly-D-lysine. A second fixation step is realised on the adhered RBCs by incubating the coverslips in osmium tetroxide in cacodylate buffer (1%) for one hour. The coverslips are then dried by incubating them in an ascending series of ethanol, then by incubating them with hexamethyldisilizane (HMDS) for 30 min. The HMDS incubation step is repeated twice before the coverslips are dried in open air. Finally, the coverslips are gold coated and imaged with a Zeiss Sigma FESEM.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare glass coverslips by incubating them in 10 % volume poly-D-lysine solution in PBS for 00:30:00 at RT
Dry and keep at 4 °C until use
Wash 100 µL of RBC in 900 µL PBS ( 00:02:00, at 1000g )
Resuspend 25 µL of the RBC suspension in 475 µL of PBS

Add 500 µL of 2 % volume glutaraldehyde solution in PBS to the cell suspension slowly (drop by drop) to reach a final glutaraldehyde concentration of 1% Work under a fume hood when using glutaraldehyde
Wash with 1 mL of PBS ( 00:02:00, at 1000g )
Dilute 100 µL of fixed cells suspension into 400 µL PBS
Incubate 200 µL of diluted cell suspension on a poly-D-lysine coated coverslip ( 01:00:00 ) in corresponding buffer (in 6-wells plates)
Rince slide 2 times with PBS (without scratching/damaging cell layer)
Incubate in 0.1 Molarity (M) cacodylate buffer ( 00:10:00 ) . Repeat step 3 times Work under a fume hood during this section, check waste disposal for the different chemicals
Post-fix with 1 Mass Percent osmium tetroxide ( 01:00:00 )

Dehydrate the sample with ethanol - Ethanol 40 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 50 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 60 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 70 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 80 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 90 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 100 % volume ( 00:15:00 ) - Ethanol 100 % volume ( 00:15:00 )
Incubate in HDMS ( 00:30:00 ). Repear step twice
Dry under the hood for 00:30:00 minimum (place filter paper under coverslip to prevent attaching to the culture plate)
Cover with a layer of gold palladium
creator: Marie Anne Balanant
